options.content:
## GatewayZ — Infrastructure & Caching Health

Core infrastructure health for the AI Inference Gateway: compute resources, Redis cache layer, and external service availability.

| Signal | Target | What to Check |
|--------|--------|---------------|
| 🖥️ **CPU** | < 80% sustained | Spike = LLM batch processing or provider retry storm |
| 💾 **Memory** | RSS < 1 GB | Growth = memory leak; check Pyroscope flamegraph |
| ⚡ **Redis Hit Rate** | > 60% | Drop = cold start or cache key churn |
| 🔗 **Redis Connections** | < 100 open | Exhaustion blocks new requests immediately |
| 🔌 **Process Health** | 0 restarts | Restart = OOM or uncaught exception |

_Metrics from Prometheus (live) · Navigate: [Four Golden Signals](#) for request SLI/SLO · [System Quality](#) for 8-pillar view · [Streaming Performance](#) for token throughput_

panels.title: 🔴  Redis Health — Memory · Evictions · Hit Ratio · Clients
Redis Hit vs Miss Rate Over Time
🔌  Connection Pool Health — Utilization · Active/Idle · Errors
Active vs Idle Connections by Pool
Circuit Breaker State Transitions Over Time
Log Ingestion Rate by Level (lines/s)
panels.description: If this panel shows no data, the backend is not pushing logs to Loki. Check LOKI_URL env var on the gatewayz-backend service.

panels.title: Log Lines Received (5m)
panels.description: Should be non-zero when backend is running. Zero = ingestion broken.

panels.title: Log Volume Trend -- Positive vs Negative Signals (INFO / WARNING / ERROR)
panels.description: A healthy system shows a high INFO baseline with minimal ERROR/CRITICAL. Growing ERROR lines relative to INFO indicate systemic degradation.

panels.title: Concurrent Requests & Queue — Active · In-Progress · Queued
panels.description: Active = passed through admission gate. Queued = waiting. If queued grows, the service is at capacity.

panels.title: Client Disconnects (499) — Requests Dropped Mid-Flight
panels.description: 499s mean the client closed the connection before the server finished. High 499 rates can indicate timeouts or oversaturated queues.

panels.title: Concurrency Gate Rejections — By Rejection Reason
panels.description: Requests rejected before processing begins. A non-zero rate means the admission gate is actively shedding load.

panels.title: 🗄️  Application Cache Performance — Hit Rate · Redis · Layer Breakdown
App Cache Hit Rate — Application-Level Cache
panels.description: Hit rate of the application-level cache (auth, model catalog, response). Drop below 50% = cold cache or high cardinality.

panels.title: Redis Hit Rate — Key-Level Cache
panels.description: Redis keyspace hit rate. Complements the app-level gauge above — Redis misses mean keys expired or were evicted.

panels.title: Cache Hits vs Misses — Application + Redis
panels.description: Green lines should dominate. Red spikes indicate cold cache or key expiry storms.

panels.title: Database Slow Traces — Click to Inspect in Tempo
panels.description: Traces matching database span patterns. Sort by Duration to find the slowest queries. Click any row to open the full waterfall.

panels.title: 📋  Operational Event Logs — Circuit Breakers · Provider Failures · Rate Limits
Circuit Breaker & Rate Limit Events — Live Log Feed
panels.description: Log lines matching circuit breaker trips, rate limit hits, and 429 throttle events. Useful during incident triage to pinpoint when a provider began failing.

panels.title: Provider Error & Timeout Logs — Errors & Warnings Only
panels.description: Log lines from provider calls that resulted in errors or timeouts. Filter by provider keyword to narrow down which upstream is failing.
